intendedUse: Official provider-backed clusters that are created, validated, benchmarked, frozen, and optionally deleted before the next provider-backed cluster is materialized.
operationalRules: Keep historical fixed-cluster VMIDs outside this range.
Delete partial provider-backed VMs before retrying a failed create operation with the same cluster_name.
Keep only one genai-pb provider-backed cluster active at a time unless cluster_name, IPs, and VMID range are intentionally changed.
Use the provider manifest and freeze artifacts, not VMID continuity, as the authoritative experiment record.

collisionAvoidance.historicalClusterProtection: The range is intentionally separated from the existing 8000-8019 environment observed during initial provider-backed testing.
collisionAvoidance.partialProvisioningHandling: If create fails after creating a subset of VMs, run the provider delete operation for cluster_name genai-pb or remove only the generated partial genai-pb VMs before retrying.
collisionAvoidance.templatePolicy: The ubuntu-24.04-template may remain at its existing VMID; vmidBase is still declared so newly generated environments have an explicit allocation boundary.
